updates.title: Claude Code v2.1.174 & v2.1.175: Enterprise model governance + 10+ bug fixes (June 12, 2026)
updates.summary: Two Claude Code releases shipped on June 12 within four hours of each other. v2.1.174 (01:16 UTC) delivers over ten bug fixes: the model picker now correctly surfaces Opus for Max/Team Premium/Enterprise plans, a false 'Fable 5 is consuming usage credits' banner no longer appears on enterprise accounts, Bedrock GovCloud regions no longer generate 400 errors due to a wrong inference profile prefix, and the VS Code extension gains a new usage attribution dashboard showing 24h/7d breakdowns by cache misses, subagents, and per-skill/agent/plugin token consumption. v2.1.175 (04:23 UTC) adds the `enforceAvailableModels` managed setting, which lets enterprise admins lock the Default model to an approved allowlist and prevent users or projects from widening that list.
updates.accuracy_notes: Data sourced directly from the official anthropics/claude-code GitHub releases page. Release timestamps (01:16 and 04:23 UTC, June 12) are machine-generated commit metadata and highly reliable. The enforceAvailableModels feature is verified as a new managed enterprise setting. No red flags or unverified claims.

updates.title: Claude Code v2.1.170 & v2.1.173: Claude Fable 5 fully integrated into the CLI (June 10-11, 2026)
updates.summary: Following Claude Fable 5's public launch on June 9, two Claude Code releases completed its CLI integration over the following 48 hours. v2.1.170 (June 10) added Fable 5 as a selectable model inside Claude Code and fixed VS Code terminal transcript saving. v2.1.173 (June 11, 05:41 UTC) refined the integration by automatically stripping the `[1m]` context-window suffix from Fable 5 model names — since 1M context is always included by default, the suffix was triggering model normalization errors. Together these two releases mark the first 48 hours of Fable 5 availability inside the developer CLI, bringing Anthropic's most capable public model into everyday coding workflows.
updates.accuracy_notes: Release dates (June 10 and June 11) confirmed from the official GitHub releases page. The Fable 5 public launch date (June 9) is corroborated by multiple independent sources including TechCrunch (2026-06-09), CNBC (2026-06-09), and the Anthropic newsroom. The [1m] suffix normalization behavior is a verbatim description from the v2.1.173 changelog. No unverified claims.
